systemic_problems: THINKING OBSESSION: Every command is 80%+ thinking blocks with minimal action
MCP DEPENDENCY HELL: Assumes external tools always work perfectly
COMPLEXITY ADDICTION: Authors can't resist adding 'just one more feature'
ABSTRACTION ABUSE: Solving theoretical problems that don't exist
VALIDATION PARANOIA: More time validating than executing
recommended_actions: DELETE 80% of all commands - they're objectively harmful
Implement HARD complexity limits (2.0 max, not 5.0)
Ban thinking blocks longer than 5 lines
Require proof of concept before any command exceeds 50 lines

methodology_validation.measurement_reliability: High - objective metrics based on file analysis
methodology_validation.bias_assessment: Minimal - numbers don't lie about complexity and length
methodology_validation.external_validation: All complexity scores exceed CLAUDE.md thresholds - objectively over-engineered

final_verdict.savage_summary: These commands are a masterclass in how to take simple tasks and make them impossibly complex. They're like using a nuclear reactor to toast bread - technically impressive, completely impractical, and likely to cause more problems than they solve. The average 30% success rate means you'd be better off flipping a coin. At least coins are faster and don't waste tokens.
